text: I don't understand why Prof. Cano has such a low score. Yes he is rigid with reports but he works with you if you ask him to. He will make you work for that 10/10 but, again, it's not bad at all. Just follow his report guidelines. His final is not bad at all too. 5/5 prof would take again.

This is probably the worst prof I have taken for LAB. He takes this one-credit class way to seriously. He does have his favorites. He makes you write a lot of stuff for each LAB. He doesn't really help when you ask for it, instead, he says to look at your LAB outline. I would suggest taking the other prof for this lab.

Not a good choice for EE's but maybe for CPE's, if you took 344 before this class then you'll have an easier time but if you havent then its best to stick with the other lab professor. Unfortunately, he demands a lot for lab reports for just a 1 credit class, should've guided us through some assembly code for some of the difficult labs.

I honestly thought the labs itself weren't that bad, its his demeanor that's a problem. He expects you to "learn" but doesn't want to point you in the right direction when you ask him questions. Then he wants lab reports that answers all the questions in the lab and then asks you why you didn't ask him questions when you submit them incomplete.

He is a lab professor but with some issues. Gets agitated really quickly if you ask questions. Has his way of teaching which is old school, wants you to work really hard for a 1 credit class that isn't that useful but somewhat knowledgeable. Please have time set out for this class because the lab reports/lab itself are a pain only for his class tho

Besides the pop quizzes, the final is the worse. I am just lost in words. Lab reports are graded very harshly. Please make sure you send .asm and. hex files separately and not on the lab report. He takes a lot of points if you merge your lab report with the assembly code.

Professor Martinez means well but by no means can teach the class properly. I had to consult professor Hoxha for everything and professor Martinez got mad at me for going to him even though he cant explain properly.

He will belittle you a lot, so get used to it. It is impossible to do the experiments on your own, as you never really learn how to code. Textbook is awful. It's only a 1Cr course and I promise it'll be your most stressful class because he just places SO much unnecessary pressure on you. He tries to compensate as he really just wants you to learn.

Not easy, but also not hard. You have to understand the codes from top to bottom so that you can ace the quizzes. He understands that the course is hard and is willing to help you finish your code. If you show interest in this subject, he will engage in serious and intersting discussions. I would recommend taking him because you learn in his class.

Very caring Professor. He cares about each student and helps to prepare for the exam by identifying topics that need to be emphasized. The class itself is not very interesting, but you will definitely learn to type faster.

Great course - and easier to understand
Great professor - I recommend

Excellent professor!!!

This Professor is useless. Cant teach at all, has a heavy accent, and doesnt seem to care
I would highly recommend this professor. You may not find a course on testing in NY area. The professor is an expert in the field. Grades are fairly given.

Actually I found this professor to be one of the best and most caring. If someone tells you not take him, it is because they do not do the projects. The course is well curved and one has to really do nothing to fail this class

Honestly this is the worse professor at city college. i am not sure if he understands well the class that himself is teaching. he can't answers questions. He always comes late, sit on his desk table, not even a marker pen to write on the board; we always have to change classroom to find him a marker so he can use. He is very disorganized.
Worst kind ever, he doesnot care, rude, never feed back, he influence you to be like him, non caring, no need to talk with him, or to attend his coarse ,,you will learn nothing

Always come late and doesnt show any interest in teaching. Have difficulty explaining class materiel. Please do yourself a favor and avoid him. This was a self-teaching class. No one really does well in exams because they do not understand.
Do yourself and your GPA favor, take someone else for EE311 or try to get an epermit to take Probability at LaGuarida CC.

I could write on about how monotonous and condescending he was, how he failed to explain lecture material clearly and just opted to read from his slides, etc. It's been some time since I took him. However, this professor contributes largely to the miserable state and poor retention of EE students at CCNY. Most everyone would agree.

I'm generally a great student (3.6 GPA) and this is not my first major specific course. I"ve taken at least EE210 before and did just fine. The ability to get partial credit on his "quizzes" is so difficult and your overall in the class can vary from exam to exam based on one question alone. Literally just take anyone else because he wont curve.

He's awesome. Extremely mechanical in his grading, but he is transparent about it, so if you want an easy A, this isn't the professor to take, but he's fair and gives everyone what they earn. Otherwise, he's a very passionate professor and a decent person, contrary to all the things other reviews have said. Mike L,
His quizzes are copy pastes from the textbook (only changes values of resistors). If you do the HW and enough textbook problems (

This man is not only a terrible professor hes also a horrible human being with no compassion. i have never seen this man smile he is straight up a villain thats only purpose is to ruin the lives of students. i beg you to never take this professor

I recommend you get an epermit for EE31100 at LaGuardia CC if you want to under probability better than golovin. Other classes are good but ee 311 is a nightmare. Take probability at LaGuardia CC, you will thank me than taking ee311 at CCNY

He's not an enthusiastic or passionate lecturer and I dreaded attending his class for his monotonous lecture. I have seen him humiliate students on occasion. Graded myself and fellow peers very harshly on quizzes and exams. Avoid.
For EE333. Only reads his lecture and recites his mistakes. Grading is rigid. Little partial credit. 4 quizzes (only got to 3), 1 mid, 1 final. Screw up any of them and it sinks your score.

Lectures are terrible, he just reads off slides in the most boring way. Quizzes and midterms are a bit of a curveball, read his questions carefully as even slight mistakes will make the question incorrect and he doesn't tend to give partial points. Very strict about attendance and a lateness counts as an absence. Avoid him if you can.

ONLY applies to EE 333, he was fun and presented the topic in a way that made me want to learn more. quizzes were mostly deriving and solving problems from lectures and tests were the same but with extra difficulty or extra questions. He is very clear about his grading and his policies and he will follow and expect you to do the same.
this creature made me question my life choices everyday. Taking his class, you might end up dropping your major.

I have taken this professor multiple times in the past and I can sincerely say he had no passion for teaching, no desire (and even annoyance) to help students, and presented course material in a way that was not beneficial to gain knowledge or to pass his exams. I would strongly suggest you take any other professor if you can.

This might be the worst professor to ever teach in any college when I say stay away I do not mean it lightly. He teaches the topic very badly and on top of that the exams have nothing to do with what he was teaching.

If you are just a second late after he finishes attendance, you may be cooked. Mainly read off the slides, which was just pasted screenshots from the textbook or straight up word per word. Very little insight on the slides but was a little helpful. Harsh and picky grading, microscopic extra credit. If you study slides and read textbook, you'll b fn

Exams worth all your grade in EE 311 and all classes @ CCNY. I suggest you try an epermit at LAGCC so you can learn probability better and earn good grades. I feel like golovin treated attendance as high school students
Currently taking him; good profesor, but it's very important to have a good grasp of physics 2

For 330, make sure you understand physics 2 well, and for 311, make sure you have taken discreet math. He will assume you know everything about those topics and may throw questions he did not go over in his slides, hw, or recommended textbook. His exams have about 5 questions and rarely gives partial credit; good luck if you choose to take Golovin.
I am never the one to drop a class (GPA > 3.8)
I recommend you try to get an E-permit for EE 31100 & EE 33000 so you can actually learn properly. If you value your GPA, E-permit is a good chance to pass and earn good grades. CCNY is a mess.

I had the displeasure of having to take this professor more than once. All I can say is that if you can, avoid avoid avoid. Unenthusiastic, rude, and does not cover the suitable material for the courses he teaches. Glad I don't have to take him again.
If you need to take ee 311, get an epermit for ee 311
Bad Attitude

Some ppl say he's rude, but imo it's his attempt at being funny. Don't get me wrong, he is super extra sometimes with the lateness. He makes you ask for permission to come into the room if you're late, but his exams are super fair. If you redo the lecture problems you will atleast get a C+ in the class. This goes for every claas the he teaches.
He inspired me to be miserable every day
This class is good with him

The class isn't easy by itself because of a lot of material, you should understand everything, otherwise it is impossible to prepare. If you made the same mistake in several questions, he would cut not once but, in all questions, where this mistake appears.6 quizzes 10% for each, midterm and one final 20% each. No curve, microscopic partial credit.

Amazing professor. One of the best I have had. Super passionate, really understanding of students, easy open book exams, and cool. No attendance required at all, and he curves a ton. You'll be super satisfied with your grade! Register for his class now, you won't regret taking this great professor!
One of the biggest CCNY memes. You either love him or hate him.
beep boop bop
He is a good professor for this course, covered material thoroughly in class. Available for office hours and gave good feedback. Attendance is definitely crucial.

Grades are purely on quizzes and tests. He realized our bad scores half way through the course and started doing open book, but by then it was too late. Gave us 1 class heads up about our midterm. His hw problems barely appear on exams, so you have to look through many problems in short amounts of time to be prepared for what he may throw at you.
HORRIBLE. I am so glad I never have to take him again. Refuses to help students.
Just no

Any class with Golovin is tough. He's not the greatest when teaching the material and gives very little partial credit. There is a quiz at the end of every chapter. It was open notes for all quizzes and exams. If you want to pass with Golovin, get past exams.
Very tough grader
Worst grader

Starts screaming at students for "distracting" him during online classes with the chat when the chat is relevant to the topic and he has to interrupt his "lesson" to read the chat anyway. The "lesson" is a slideshow copied from the textbook but with more errors and is read slowly and with less care and effort than it takes to clear one's throat.

You have to show up to this class or you will get kicked out. No more than 3 absences and you're withdrawn from the class. 6 quizzes, a midterm and a final but I highly suggest you to study the material and learn Mathematica as that will save you. Lectures are boring but he will call on you if he think you're not paying attention and will roast you

This is now my second semester of Golovin. I would recommend him for online because I got an A- in both his classes. He can be extremely mean at times and will most likely insult you if you miss class or are late. Grading breakdown is transparent from day 1. No homeworks. No curving. Attendance is critical. Good luck

Golovin is a crazy prof. He bullies kids but u need to be smart or u will fail and get kicked out his class. Study his slides read the textbook and 100 PERCENT DO HIS HOMEWORK!!!! Golovin is crazy on quizes like he make some hard some easy and he takes so much points off. Never going to see gologvin ever again.

Golovin just reads off the slides and you will not learn anything during lectures. He will not drop any quizzes and he will not curve. He calls on people on random during class so beware if you actually do take him. You will be kicked out of the class if you get 3 absences.

Golovin is soo smart that he was the special ability to make things 100 times harder than what it actually is. His excellent teaching inspired me to just read off slides in a deep voice for 30 years while forcing his students to come to lecture. In addition, he only gives exams/quizzes which btw are not curved, so if you mess up "too bad".

He is the WORST professor I have ever had. He is mean & disrespectful. Your Grades goes down drastically because of his grading technique. He is a difficult person & will never hear out student's concerns. NO CURVE!

He's decent, the class is mostly the textbook and online curriculum. His lectures would be good but 1)he talks the physics,curriculum doesn't care, 2) pace is slow,often lags behind the HW schedule, 3) his English is rough,doesn't always understand student questions. One very hard and novel Q on final worth 25%,no partial credit, don't expect "A"

He is the WORST! Very inconsiderate & will not own his mistakes on online tests. He is mean & disrespectful. He changed the syllabus midway & made 5 quizzes worth 80%. Get a few points off on 1 test? Your A goes to a B/B- bc each test is 16%! He is a difficult person & will never hear out student's concerns. NO CURVE! HE EVEN ROUNDS GRADES DOWN!

Gives lots of quizes and 1 final exam, homework is given but not taken into account but i HIGHLY recommend doing the hw before the quizes because a lot of the questions were similar to the hw. He seems to be in a very monotone and grouchy mood in class and asks everyone in class if we have questions if no one replies. Study hard and you'll survive.

Hes always angry.. Hes very petty and holds grudges all semester on the smallest inconveniences. His class requires near perfection to do well. Thankfully I was able to get a hundred on the final and got an A-, otherwise each wrong question wouldve dropped me multiple letter grades down. I did not enjoy hearing his voice at 11am at all..

This professor is a fraud. Initially, he told us that he is going to take only 8 quizzes each weighing 10%. He intentionally proceeds slowly to complete the syllabus and took only 5 quizzes. If the final answer doesn't match with him then it cuts off 20-30 points from 100. His slides doesn't contain detailed explanations.

Terrible professor. He changed the number of quizzes we take from the original 8 down to 5 and now each one is worth 16% of our grade. We all asked for him to reconsider the grading criteria from 80% for quizzes and 20% for homeworks for something else or at least dropping the lowest grade since everyone is struggling and this asshat said no.

My grade went from an A- to a C+ because of a two question on the final exam. There are no partial credits because a computer grades the answers. I had a 98 average on quizzes and then messed up on two questions on the final. Also the two questions i messed up were not topics we focused on nor did did he say would be on the exam. Avoid him.

I took this professor for the second time. He mostly test you on derivations of law of optics and Electromagnetic waves. Due to covid-19 he start to test people based on calculations. If you study his notes properly, you will be fine. Good luck!

Not bad, he gives only 6 quizzes all about previous lectures. Midterm and Final are also about previous lectures. He gives the list of questions for midterm-final, but he changes some parameters on the exams. No memorization, bc problems would not be exactly similar to lectures. It is hard but can be done with some practice.

This professor use Connect online assignments from McGraw-Hill. Assignments are not complicated, but require some practice. Be aware of a half-point policy, which is not that bad. He gives only half-point if you make a mathematical mistake on midterm/final. He is very strict on grades, he takes grades directly from black-board, no curving.

He is semi-decent and if you want an A, you must memorize his lecture to the finest. He has a depressive monotone, does answer questions, and is helpful during office hours. He is not the best but he is not the worst. Thus, I would take him and you should memorize heavily to do well in his class. Not a bad person.

attendance was taken every lecture. group project was part of final grade. quizzes were deductions from his lectures. midterm and final were hard and computer model was needed to pass. in overall it was hard b/c his tough grading demands to learn all small pieces of lectures.
quizzes on Connect are easy jokes, midterm and final are real deals, above all no curving!
A lot of mathematics proofs, easy quizzes but hard midterm and final.

The material is actually interesting, but this guy will sap any and all life out of the class, and his grading policy is RIDICULOUS. Any other professor would take 5 points off MAX for miscalculations, in this class you will lose 20+ points and drop a whole letter grade. Buy some books and read on your own, this guys is not worth it.
Clearly has no interest in teaching. Automatically lose half credit if you get a wrong answer, which is nuts when the exam has only three questions.
I took 4 courses with him. Decent professor, he let me to express my opinion and doubts.

I used his notes from the black-board. His quizzes are tricky, midterm and final are similar to lectures, but more difficult b/c he changes assignments. He takes final grades straight from the black-board, does not curve grades, always checks attendance

Got C for his 204, but A+ for 339 Bc it is open notes. 333 tests are closed (only 1 minute for tests) so avoiding it. NO mistakes get a A, mistakes lose half points get a C. Dont rely just on notes, read, do HW, and understand Bc he changes variables on quiz and test(exact same as HW), simple if you read, confusing if not. Would take ONLY for 339.

He teaches lectures of EE339 Semiconductors along K-handbook. Attendance is required, half points off for mathematics mistakes. Make sure that you follow his reviews of home assignments. The open lectures notes midterm and final are manageable.

EE333 continues his EE330. Gives quizzes every second week, easy questions about last lectures. Gives small homework and checks it in the class. He gives questions and problems for midterm and final. Midterm and final are very different from quizzes, can be tricky. All assignments only about his lectures, he gives 1 minute to look lecture notes.

He taught EE339 by using handbook. He goes by the pattern - attendance, lecture, review questions, homework, open book quiz. Midterm and final are based on the review questions and homework, calculators and open lecture notes only. Takes off 50% of points for math mistakes. With no mistakes you are A+ victorious.

Its so funny he teaches this class without even mentioning about Electromagnetics. He has zero knowledge of Electromagnetics. He treats this class as a High School optics class. He brings up some formulas which has no physical meanings. No one really understand what he is doing. If you are good at memorizing things than take him. Else take other.

Hes the worst professor ever! Dont even think about taking him! His exams are so tricky and he doesnt give partial credit its either right or wrong. I ended up learning nothing in that class and it ruined my gpa so bad.

i simply do not know how to describe a horrible person like that. he is, i can say, a trash bag that you would like to dispose in the nearest garbage area as soon as you can. that how i felt while taking his class. 1 question in midterm and final can move you from A to C or even D. avoid taking this maniac who does not know what he is doing

Class upto first midterm is very easy. Most of the people get 100% on quiz and test because it's super easy stuff. But after 1st mid-term , the materials he teaches contains lots of mistakes. And also lot of start to lose lots of points because the equations he provide will have no physical meanings. So i would simply suggest not to take his class
Lets make it easy and short AVOID HIM

This one of the boring class. He taught all the stuff i learned in high school optics. He makes lots of error, even in the test question and when you try to correct he does not listen. He punished student for the mistakes he makes. Horrible prof. You can easily slide from A to F in matter of no time. Avoid if you can.

I took EE339 with him. Classes were given along Kano's handbook. Material of the book is not easy, but prof. provided us with a lot of comments on each paragraph and solving the end-chapter problems in the classroom. He ended each chapter with a quiz with problems similar to home-works. If you do not skip his classes, you should not have problems.
I had to study hard. If needed, he will help.

Straight to business. Cons: tough grader, no partial credit, midterm and the finals have only 3 or 4 question so be careful, mandatory attendance, Dont be late(assumed absent after he finishes calling names), Heavy Russian accent, no curving. pros: class contents are fairly easy, goes thru problems in the class, Quizzes are 50%.

Class itself is easy. However, he tests you on stuff that are so unimportant to the cource that you barely cover 10 minutes of it in class. Stuff such as deriving formulas. I spend a week studying circuits for the midterm and he gave questions on deriving formulas and node analysis (which we just learnt). No Partial. makes TON of mistakes in class.

midterm and final only. Tests are 3 questions each. no partial credit. Tests on unimportant things are we barely cover (ex deriving already memorized formulas that we can easily search up in the real world) Final was on topics we barely covered. We told us to study op amps by our selves and said it was going to be on final. Messes up in class.

I am editing my review after taking classes that have 204 as a prereq. Golovin does not cover source transforms, Thevenin/Norton, or OP amps in any appreciable detail. You will need to learn all of these things on your own to do well in 205 and 241. His tests are 3 questions, not hard, but stingy with partial.

Do not skip his lectures, he describes all material for midterm and final in the class. He knows optics and microwave, so you actually can learn in this class. Tough grader, no excuses for even small mistakes.

He is an outstanding lecturer, but very strict on the final grades. Be aware of his half-points policy, mistakes in calculations will cost 50% of points and another 50 for mistakes in formulas. It is actually better than the Connect grading. Midterm and final contain 3 problems each, no cell phones or handbook allowed.

He teaches 204 quite slow at the beginning. He gives time to physics , but then he goes faster towards end of the course. Lectures are good, be careful do not delay on quizzes or he walks away without your work. Always checks an attendance. He grades by the value of weighted total from the blackboard, he does not curve. A-B are plausible.

He is good lecturer and very organized. If you had him in EE330, then you learn in the similar way through mathematical derivations. He is very strict on the mathematics, you cannot make any mistake. He provides lists of questions for the midterm and final. A and F, both are possible.

This class is not difficult, but requires an additional work at home. He gives a lot of proves with mathematics and computer models. Better to attend all lectures, quizzes can be tricky, midterm and final are not accumulative. A is possible but requires a perfect work.
Be ready for an extra work in his class. No extra credits and only half partial-credits on the midterm and final. Assignments are not complicated but require a lot of practice. Lectures are good.

Doesnt care for student development. Your grade is purely based on statistical calculation. Onlinehw quiz system was annoying and made some errors to the grading of my hw so watch out for it. Test questions are straight forward but DONT MAKE ANY MISTAKES. Little partial credit. Midterm class grades was either A or F. Takes attendance every lecture.

Must be perfect if you choose to be in this class. A 3 question midterm and final. The last two questions decide whether or not you receive a A or F. You use wrong units, variable, or forget to add a number then say goodbye to most of the points for the question because he gives at most 5-10 points for right work process. A or fail, no between.

Absolutely a harsh grader. You can have the right concept or idea but If one of your calculations are wrong(i.e. missing number or wrong sign) then he will take off most of the points. Very little partial credit. He started using Mcgraw hill connect and it has glitches and you cannot get partial credit on it. Test questions are from lecture.

PLEASE DO TAKE ANOTHER CLASS INSTEAD OF OPTICS THIS GUY IS SO ARROGANT AND POOR AT TEACHING MIDTERM AND FINAL IS 30% EACH IF YOU MESSED UP WITH ONE TEST YOUR GRADE WILL BE A TO C REAL QUICK AND SOMETIMES I DONT UNDERSTAND WHAT HE IS TRYING TO SAY BECAUSE OF HES THICK ACCENT ONE OF MY BAD EXPERIENCE IN CCNY SCHOOL LIFE

Not a bad teacher, but he gives 3 question exams and is very stingy for partial, even if your concept is correct but you made a calcularion mistake. Most people get A's or F's on the midterm. Nothing really in between. That said, the questions themselves are not that hard, and are lifted directly out of lecture.

It was a terrible experience. Our class was well behind the other ENGR204 classes. He spent the first few weeks on the physics of electricity, and the last two weeks he rushed through 5 chapters because he ran out of time. McGraw Hill Connect will screw you over, and his tests are only 3 questions out of laziness. I wish I had learned something

He is very consistent instructor, explaining important details of every concept. I strongly recommend taking his classes. I took 3 of his EE classes (Engr 204, EE 330, EE 333) and my experience is "superior"

Teavhes very slowly and clearly, almost too slowly at times. Tests are very, very easy but he is harsh on partial credit. You do both HW and Quizzes on an online system called "connect". The hw assignments are quite difficuly compared to what is done in class or on the tests

One of the very best in EE. He's a man of procedure. If you follow his procedure, you're golden. He goes strictly by his grading policy outlined in the syllabus. The proofs he does in class are insightful because they teach you how to think. Memorizing proofs without understanding is a proven disaster to your grade. You've been warned!
A wonderful professor. He is very approachable and explains things if you don't understand.

He's one of the few profs who greets his students. He teaches concepts slowly and in depth to make sure everyone understands. His quizzes and exams aren't hard but they must be done with extreme caution. No tolerance for calculation mistakes. If your concept is correct and you mess up calculations, you'll just receive half credit. Good Professor.

Had him for only half of the summer semester. He was consistent with his lectures and his quizzes weren't overwhelming. His grading is strict. He expects perfection. Best thing about him is he expects students to know only the questions and concepts that he teaches in class. The class material was boring but he made it manageable! A/B is possible.

if you know how to understand the concepts and memorize stuffs then this is the guy to take but be aware of his grading; one mistake your done. most of the exams and quizzes are covered from the lecture. the exams are straight forward if you give time to his notes and its easy to pass and fail this class. organized and consistent professor.

I would pass on this professor. He does not give much partial credit, and he does not finish the whole curriculum. Did not finish the last three chapters. He showed in the beginning his past two classes grades, for one half failed and for the other half dropped the class. It is possible to get an A, but one mistake and you get a 70 on the exam.
If you have to take him, my honest advice is attend every lectures and study them. Extra effort needed!! good luck!

One of the worst egoistic man i have met in my life. Give easy exam but expect you to do perfectly since he gives like absolutly No partial at all. Therefore, you are either getting an A or F on the exam. make sure u have to study the his note every week. b/c he gives the pop quizzes based on that. if you bombed it, good luck to you

Moody and not interested in student's development. Shows unmistakable boredom at office hours visits. Clings to statistics greatly, which makes his job easier for him. Will not curve, even if half a percent is needed. He probably lets system calculate the grade without any interference from his side. His exams are literally memorizing and copying.

He doesn't expect students to know anything outside of his teachings for the quizzes and exams. Bad partial credit, so do things in the EXACT WAY he does. Before midterm and final, he gives a list of possible questions. If there's an unclear question, make sure you ask for clarification since it can be on the test. An A is possible with practice.

Great professor, you will learn in this class. Except, the exams are horrible, nothing like quizzes. DOES NOT give any partial credit. Beware, exams are more of deriving formulas than applying the information learned (same way he derives formulas in class). Textbook is straight forward and will help you to further understand material.
The quizes, midterms are exactly the class lectures. Nothing out of ordinary, straight forward.

This is the sort of class where you can get an A without having to understand any of the material the prof scribbles on the blackboard - provided you memorize it all. And that is how this class is taught, as an exercise in memorization.

The class at time can go at a very slow pace but that is because he wants to make sure you understand his lectures. The exams are very straight forward. Expect proofs for the quizzes and the exams. Very nice professor as well during the office hours.
Best professor in CCNY. His exams is mostly about proofs he already gave in class. He will give you the sets of questions ahead of time. He will choose some questions from that set.

Rest in peace if you are a CPE major. If you're EE, find another elective. Kim gives project that are very time consuming. EE457 requires all you got to give to survive. I wish have taken engr 204 with someone or epermit to bmcc/Laguardia instead of Kim. Take my advice instead of learning things the hard way. Very brutal professor at CCNY
Run away! Will Ruin your GPA.
Why he is still there is beyond me.
Why is he still teaching, it's beyond me.

EE 457 with Bruce Kim is extremely time consuming. Lectures are often unclear, and much of the material must be self-taught. Exams don't always match what's covered. Be ready to rely on outside resources and invest significant time to pass. CPE majors, stay strong

If being a bad professor was an Olympic sport, Bruce Kim would have a gold medal. His inability to teach can be felt from the very first lecture. It's clear that he has outside issues that he is projecting onto his students. Avoid like the plague if you care about your sanity and GPA
How does this guy still have a job. I am certain all positive reviews are written by him.

This professor literally reads the slides like it's his first time seeing them. He is honestly not competent. If you wanna learn, avoid him, and if you care about your gpa, avoid him. he's just bad in every measurable sense.

Rest in peace if you're a computer engineering major. Ee 45700 demands all you have what it takes to survive. I have nightmares of having Bruce Kim as an instructor. If you need to take engr20400, epermit at bmcc or LAGCC or Queens college to save your gpa. The emperor has no clothes
His woman wasn't loyal, now we all have to deal with him. A bitter old man who wants everyone to be as bitter as he is.
I wish the worst on Bruce Kim
If you need this class in ur schedule, either find another professor or don't take it at all
As bad as it gets.
